title: E2E: padded message delivery roundtrip integration test
description:
What: Integration test in relay/tests/e2e_jsonrpc.rs verifying that a message padded by the sender arrives intact at the receiver after unpadding.

Algorithm:
1. spawn_relay() (existing helper)
2. Connect Alice and Bob (two transport connections)
3. Wait for DirectoryList on both
4. Alice constructs a known payload: b"hello from alice" (16 bytes)
5. Alice pads it: padded = pad(b"hello from alice").unwrap()
6. Alice sends BroadcastParams { payload: padded }
7. Bob receives DeliverParams; extracts deliver.payload
8. Bob asserts deliver.payload.len() is one of {256, 512, 1024, 4096} (bucket-aligned)
9. Bob unpadded = unpad(&deliver.payload).unwrap()
10. Assert: unpadded == b"hello from alice"

Also test rejection of oversized payload:
11. Alice constructs a 4093-byte payload (over the 4092 byte limit)
12. pad(&payload) must return Err (assert Err at call site, NOT a relay test)

Oracle:
- pad(b"hello from alice") where len=16: 4+16=20 < 256, so bucket=256. Output is 256 bytes with first 4 bytes = [16,0,0,0].
- unpad of that output returns exactly b"hello from alice"
- This is verifiable from first principles without the code under test

Defensive rules:
- Use real pad/unpad calls (not mock) to test the actual implementation
- Do not use rand in this test — use a fixed known payload for determinism

Team: standard R+I+T
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-relay --test e2e_jsonrpc -- padded passes; deliver.payload.len() is exactly 256; unpad gives back original bytes
